rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1827 33rd Ave, San Francisco, Ca, 94122)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues: (1) Proof of publication appears defective: (a) The name of the case not accurately set forth: "In the Matter of the Estate of Thaddeus Edward Loutcheck, Conservbatee" is not the case name. (b) The beginning date of offer is listed after 3/25/24; however, this private sale occurred on 3/18/24. (c) The terms of sale do not mirror what Attachment 4f sets forth. Also, the publication indicates " acceptable to the personal representative." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Off calendar

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: (2) Continued to 7/2/24. Continued to 7/2/24 Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) When did Francis (or Frances) Trammell Coleman die? When did Donald Trammell die? ("Now deceased" is not elucidating.) Since the SNT has not been established, how can the Court determine who is entitled to the estate (or non-existent trust)? Paragraph 16 of the petition also asserts that tax returns "are to be filed on behalf of the estate and its sole beneficiary, the Special Needs Trust." (4) Under what authority can the Court disregard the terms of the will that created a SNT? (5) A petition for approval of an account or a report accompanying the petition shall contain all the statements required by Probate Code Section 1064(a). (6) The petition fails to disclose whether or not notice was required by, or given pursuant to, Prob. C. Section 9202(b). (7) What does the executor propose to do with the $3,836,234.52 sums ordered to be returned or paid by Lawrence Porchia? Should that amount not be distributed? (8) This Court does not ordinarily award more than $45/hour to non-professional fiduciaries absent some showing that the services warranted a higher amount. The executor's "expense report," furthermore, does not show the specific hours spent. (9) An accounting for a decedent's estate must begin with the I&A value; however, given the unfortunate circumstances of this estate, the accounting will likely not need to be amended, but statements supporting the beginning balances would be helpful. (The time sheets have not yet been reviewed in detail.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 6/18/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/4/24 for the attorney to appear on the following issues: 1) Attorney was advised that Probate Code 9202(b) was revised to require notice to the California Victim Compensation Board even if heirs have been previously confined. The revised allegation made in the 2nd amendment filed on 5/15/24 does not address whether the heir was previously confined. 2) Attorney was advised that a waiver of account needed to be filed by Greg Yee as administrator of the Estate of Diana Yee. Instead, waivers of account have been filed by the alleged heirs of the Estate of Diana Yee. 3) Notice of Hearing on filing the Second Amendment was served on 5/1/24; however, the Second Amendment was filed on 5/15/24, and notice of filing a document cannot be given if the document has not already been filed. Furthermore, the Second Amendment was signed by the attorney and petitioner on 5/9/24; thus it appears that Notice was given even prior to execution of the document. 4) The proposed order lodged on 5/17/24 is deficient, including: a. Does not specify all assets on hand (including securities and automobile) and does not delineate the specific assets that will be distributed. b. States that Decedent died testate, which is incorrect. c. Misstates the amount of reserve (which was changed to $2,000 in the Second Amendment). d. Misstates the cash on hand ($2,683.29), which was alleged to be $2,183.29 in paragraph 4 of the Second Amendment.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of diligence, as the following issues have not been addressed since this petition was initially set for 11/01/2023 and since counsel's appearance at the last hearing on 05/01/2024: 1) No DE-147 form is on file. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). In addition, the following issues have not been addressed since the amended petition was filed on 04/04/2024 and since counsel's last (and third) appearance: 1) Re the DE-121 form filed on 04/04/2024: i) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) was not attached. ii) Item 1 must be completed. iii) The word "AMENDED" (petition) must be inserted where applicable. 2) Re the AMENDED petition filed on 04/04/2024: i) Item 5a(2) was not checked. ii) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. iii) The decedent died more than 4 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. Note: The Court notes that the only progress shown after the last hearing on 05/01/2024 is submission of a proposed order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MANNERS FAMILY TRUST DATED MARCH 1, 2019
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petitioner is not the only party entitled to notice, as alleged in paragraph 13. [CRC 7.902.] A supplement to the petition must be filed, setting forth the name and address of the contingent remainder beneficiary. (2) The proof of service indicates that an incomplete copy of the petition (without exhibits) was served. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/18/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
